commit.message:
fix(cmi): correct sample playback speed — was 11x too slow ("whale")

Root cause: CMI hardware pitch model reads wave_ram at ~1220 samples/sec
for middle C, designed for 128-sample page loops. Real Fairlight samples
at 14080 Hz would play at 0.087x speed — 13 seconds for a 1-second sample.

Fix: Added sample_mode flag — when a sample >256 bytes is loaded,
processSample() uses linear addressing with sample_inc = playRate/sampleRate
where playRate = 14080 * (freq/261.63). Hardware page-stepping mode preserved
for built-in waveforms.

Verified: 261 Hz output for MIDI 60 with 14080 Hz test sine (ratio 0.998x).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Smooth pattern editor scrolling for GT Ultra playback

Added FormatPlaybackState singleton — a lightweight format-agnostic
playback state that any format engine can write to. The
PatternEditorCanvas RAF loop reads it every frame (no React props in
the hot path) and computes smooth sub-pixel scroll offset using
time-based interpolation, identical to normal mode.

Changes:
- New: FormatPlaybackState.ts — tracks row, timestamp, rolling-average
  row duration for smooth interpolation
- GTUltra worklet: increase position update rate from 32 blocks (~93ms)
  to 2 blocks (~5.8ms) for responsive row tracking
- useGTUltraEngineInit: pipes position callbacks to FormatPlaybackState
- FT2Toolbar: sets FormatPlaybackState playing on play/stop
- PatternEditorCanvas: format mode RAF loop reads FormatPlaybackState
  directly (not React props) and computes smoothOffset per frame

fix: correct SurfaceNormal BVH face selection and add SN mismatch CI guard (#269)

* fix: correct SurfaceNormal BVH face selection and add SN mismatch CI guard

Phase 2 of the SurfaceNormal optimization (commit 7f1e867) used a pure
BVH triangle-to-face mapping (BVHFindNearestFaceBounds / fTriangleFaceIdx)
to identify the nearest face in O(log N_triangles).  This is fundamentally
incorrect near face boundaries: the tessellation error fBVHDeflection
(= 0.01 × max_face_bbox_diagonal) means the nearest tessellated triangle
may belong to a different analytical face than the true nearest face.
For g4tubs this produced 1275/2048 wrong normals; summed across all curved
fixtures the benchmark reported 13 937 SurfaceNormal mismatches.

Fix (Phase 2):
  Replace BVHFindNearestFaceBounds with BVH-seeded TryFindClosestFace:
  - BVHLowerBoundDistance(p) gives mesh lower bound bvhLB (~0 for a
    surface point).
  - seedDist = bvhLB + 2*fBVHDeflection is a valid upper bound on the
    true distance to the nearest surface point.
  - TryFindClosestFace(fFaceBoundsCache, p, seedDist) prunes distant
    faces via AABB before the BRepExtrema call, then uses exact
    BRepExtrema on all candidates — guaranteeing the correct face is
    identified regardless of tessellation approximation errors.

  Add maxDistance parameter to TryFindClosestFace (default kInfinity,
  fully backward-compatible) so callers can supply a BVH-derived upper
  bound as an initial pruning threshold.

  Remove BVHFindNearestFaceBounds, fTriangleFaceIdx (no longer needed;
  fTriangleSet is still used by BVHLowerBoundDistance).

CI guard:
  bench_navigator already returned EXIT_FAILURE when the ValidationReport
  contained SN mismatch errors, but only up to max_reported_mismatches (8)
  errors per fixture were added.  Add an explicit check on the summed
  surface_normal_mismatch_count across all fixtures so that any non-zero
  SN mismatch total causes EXIT_FAILURE, independent of the ValidationReport
  error-propagation path.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: add explicit CI guards for all correctness mismatch counts in bench_navigator

The existing HasErrors(aggregate_report) check is the primary guard, but
each ValidationReport carries at most max_reported_mismatches (8) errors per
fixture, leaving the raw per-fixture counts as the only complete record.
Three specific gaps existed beyond the SurfaceNormal mismatch check added
in the previous commit:

- Exit-normal mismatches (normal_mismatch_count) share the combined
  mismatch_count cap with intersection/distance mismatches; if 8
  intersection errors exhaust the cap, exit-normal errors receive zero
  ValidationReport entries, making HasErrors() blind to them.
- Ray intersection/distance and Inside(p) mismatches both carry at least
  one ValidationReport error per fixture with any mismatch, but rely on
  the same uncertain error-propagation path that failed to catch the SN
  regression (commit 7f1e867).

Fix: after PrintNavigationReport, sum the raw mismatch counts from
state.summaries (skipping expected-failure fixtures whose errors are
legitimately reclassified to warnings) and return EXIT_FAILURE if any
non-zero count is found, printing one error line per affected method.
Also update the SurfaceNormal check from the previous commit to exclude
expected-failure fixtures consistently.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Tested with session_id=46:

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Normalize worktree paths in RepoRegistry to prevent cache poisoning

When a worktree at .agent-workspaces/workspace-XYZ was registered, it
overwrote the correct repo root in the urlToPath/hashToPath maps. This
caused getLocalPath(for:) to return worktree paths, which the scheduler
then passed as workingDirectory to chains.run — leading to double-nested
worktrees and chains targeting wrong directories.

Fix: registerRepo() and registerRepo(remoteURL:localPath:) now strip
.agent-workspaces/ suffixes before storing, ensuring the canonical repo
root is always used.

commit.message:
Fix GT Ultra pattern editor not following playback

useGTUltraFormatData was reading isPlaying/currentRow from
useTransportStore (the standard tracker engine transport), but GT Ultra
has its own WASM engine with its own playback state. The transport store
values were always false/0 during GT Ultra playback.

Now reads playing and playbackPos.row from useGTUltraStore, which is
correctly updated by the worklet's position callback every 32 frames.

commit.message:
Fix GT Ultra silent playback: initialize editorInfo in gt_play()

gt_play() didn't initialize editorInfo.maxSIDChannels, adparam, or
multiplier before starting the sequencer. When .sng files lack the
ID=0x1f extended info block, these stay at 0 (the C global default).

With maxSIDChannels=0, the playroutine() channel loop in gplay.c
skips ALL channels — no notes are sequenced, producing a brief thud
(initial SID gate) then silence.

Added the same safety defaults already used by gt_export_prg/sid:
  maxSIDChannels=3, adparam=0x0f00, multiplier=1

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: scaffold full-stack Nextra app (Next.js + C++ Drogon)

Complete greenfield scaffold for a production-quality gamified web app
with AI chat integration, backed by a high-performance C++ Drogon API
compiled to a native binary.

Frontend (Next.js 16 + TypeScript):
- MUI with MD3 design tokens, dark/light mode via colorSchemes API
- Redux Toolkit + RTK Query + Redux Persist
- next-intl i18n (en/es) with proxy.ts locale routing
- Atomic design: 9 atoms, 8 molecules, 11+ organisms (<100 LOC each)
- 11 custom hooks (useAuth, useAiChat, useGamification, etc.)
- Full accessibility: data-testid, aria-*, tabIndex, keyboard shortcuts
- Constants in JSON, JSDoc on exports, strict TypeScript

Backend (C++ Drogon + Conan 2):
- 6 controllers (Auth, Users, Gamification, Notifications, Chat, Health)
- 7 services (Auth, Email/SMTP, Gamification, Notifications, AI, Migration, User)
- 3 filters (JWT auth, CORS, rate limiting)
- PostgreSQL with 8-table schema + SQL migrations + JSON seed data
- CLI11 entry point (serve, migrate, seed, create-admin subcommands)
- jwt-cpp, mailio, nlohmann/json, spdlog, fmt

Tooling (C++, zero Python/shell):
- CMake generator (inja + JSON + filesystem glob)
- Project manager CLI (build, test, docker, lint, fmt, generate, migrate, seed)
- clang-format + clang-tidy configs

DevOps:
- Multi-stage Dockerfiles for both services
- Docker Compose (dev + prod)
- CapRover captain-definition files
- GitHub Actions: CI, Docker/GHCR, release, CodeQL, deploy, dependabot
- CODEOWNERS, PR template, dependency review

Documentation:
- README, CLAUDE.md, agents.md, roadmap.md, plan.md
- docs/api.md, docs/deployment.md, docs/architecture.md

Normalize worktree paths in project path resolution

When chains.run fell back to resolvedLastWorkingDirectory, it could
return a .agent-workspaces/workspace-* path instead of the actual repo
root. This caused chains to target worktree directories and show
incorrect paths in the UI Project field.

Fix:
- Add AgentManager.normalizeRepoPath() that strips .agent-workspaces/
  suffixes to recover the parent repo root
- Apply normalization in the lastUsedWorkingDirectory setter (prevents
  persisting worktree paths)
- Apply normalization in resolvedLastWorkingDirectory getter (catches
  stale values from UserDefaults)
- Apply normalization in chains.run handler as belt-and-suspenders

commit.message:
feat: freshness scoring, brain prune, brain restore

Freshness scoring engine (multiplicative model):
- Formula: min(recency × usageBoost × volatilityModifier, 1.0)
- Recency: exponential decay with 60-day half-life (base signal)
- Usage: logarithmic boost multiplier (1.0-2.0x, never resurrects dead docs)
- Volatility: content-type adjustment (stable: 1.0, neutral: 0.82, volatile: 0.7)
- Scores cached in SQLite with PRAGMA-based schema migration

brain prune: archives stale entries to _archive/ based on freshness threshold.
--dry-run, --threshold, --force, --min-age, --include-type flags.
Uses gray-matter spread pattern to avoid reference mutation bugs.

brain restore: moves entries from _archive/ back to original location.
--force, --list flags. Reverses prune operation cleanly.

Integrations: sync recalculates freshness after rebuild. list shows
freshness indicators. --stale/--fresh filters on list.

63 new tests (31 freshness, 12 prune, 10 restore, 10 schema/stats).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Persist matrix animation state across runs and align to terminal top (#343)

The matrix animation was reset on every `App::new()` and its coordinates
were viewport-relative, causing it to jump/restart between prompts
instead of appearing stationary against the terminal background.

## Changes

**State persistence**
- Removed `reset_matrix_anim_state()` call from `App::new()` — the
global `MATRIX_ANIM_STATE` now lives for the terminal session, not
per-prompt invocation
- Removed the now-dead `reset_matrix_anim_state` function

**Terminal-absolute coordinates**
- `apply_matrix_anim` now takes `viewport_top: u16` and
`terminal_height: u16`
- `state.update()` receives `terminal_height` so tendril `y`-positions
are in terminal-absolute rows (0 = terminal top)
- When writing tendrils into the content buffer, rows above
`viewport_top` are skipped via `.skip(viewport_top as usize)`; buffer
index = `term_row - viewport_top`

**Buffer sizing**
- Buffer is extended to `terminal_height - viewport_top` rows (fills
viewport-top → terminal-bottom), replacing the hardcoded `40`
- Early return when `viewport_top >= terminal_height` (no visible area)

**Call-site plumbing**
- `create_content` gains `viewport_top` and `terminal_height` params
- Run loop passes `frame_area.y` and `last_terminal_area.height`

commit.message:
fix: restore Tailwind CSS loading and add mobile hamburger menu (#65)

- Fix CSS reference in App.razor: css/input.css → css/app.css
  The Tailwind CLI compiles Styles/input.css to wwwroot/css/app.css,
  but the HTML was referencing the source file path which doesn't
  exist in wwwroot, causing zero CSS to load.

- Add mobile hamburger menu to NavMenuComponent
  The nav used hidden md:flex with no mobile fallback, making the
  entire navigation invisible on screens < 768px. Added a hamburger
  toggle button (visible on mobile only) and a dropdown panel with
  vertical nav links that auto-closes on navigation.

- Fix inaccurate text on Home page: 'on the left' → 'above'

[Repo Assist] fix: show default parameter values concisely in signature help (#1512)

* fix: show default parameter values concisely in signature help (#1310)

When a C#-style optional parameter is decorated with
[<Optional; DefaultParameterValue(value)>], signature help previously
showed the full verbose attribute syntax, e.g.:

  [<System.Runtime.InteropServices.Optional;
    System.Runtime.InteropServices.DefaultParameterValue("")>] args: string

Now the attribute block is stripped and the default value is shown inline:

  args: string = ""

If no DefaultParameterValue is present (only [<Optional>]), the attribute
is silently removed leaving just the clean name: type display.

Implementation: add TipFormatter.cleanParameterDisplay which uses a regex
to detect [<...>] blocks, extract any DefaultParameterValue argument, and
reconstruct the parameter string. Use it in TextDocumentSignatureHelp
parameter documentation.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* ci: trigger checks

* test: add unit tests for TipFormatter.cleanParameterDisplay

Cover the following scenarios:
- Full verbose C# interop attributes (Optional + DefaultParameterValue) with string default
- Short-form Optional+DefaultParameterValue with string default
- Numeric default value
- Boolean default value
- Optional-only (no DefaultParameterValue) — attribute stripped, no suffix
- Plain parameter with no attribute — returned unchanged
- Multiple tagged-text segments concatenated before processing

All 7 new tests pass alongside the 8 pre-existing TipFormatter tests.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
